title: Sutherland占퐏 Grave
content:
혻혻혻혻All night long the sea out yonder all night long the wailful sea,
혻혻혻혻Vext of winds and many thunders, seeketh rest unceasingly!
혻혻혻혻Seeketh rest in dens of tempest, where, like one distraught with pain,
혻혻혻혻Shouts the wild-eyed sprite, Confusion seeketh rest, and moans in vain:
혻혻혻혻Ah! but you should hear it calling, calling when the haggard sky
혻혻혻혻Takes the darks and damps of Winter with the mournful marsh-fowl占퐏 cry;
혻혻혻혻Even while the strong, swift torrents from the rainy ridges come
혻혻혻혻Leaping down and breaking backwards million-coloured shapes of foam!
혻혻혻혻Then, and then, the sea out yonder chiefly looketh for the boon
혻혻혻혻Portioned to the pleasant valleys and the grave sweet summer moon:
혻혻혻혻Boon of Peace, the still, the saintly spirit of the dew-dells deep 
혻혻혻혻Yellow dells and hollows haunted by the soft, dim dreams of sleep.

혻혻혻혻All night long the flying water breaks upon the stubborn rocks 
혻혻혻혻Ooze-filled forelands burnt and blackened, smit and scarred with lightning shocks;
혻혻혻혻But above the tender sea-thrift, but beyond the flowering fern,
혻혻혻혻Runs a little pathway westward pathway quaint with turn on turn 
혻혻혻혻Westward trending, thus it leads to shelving shores and slopes of mist:
혻혻혻혻Sleeping shores, and glassy bays of green and gold and amethyst!
혻혻혻혻There tread gently gently, pilgrim; there with thoughtful eyes look round;
혻혻혻혻Cross thy breast and bless the silence: lo, the place is holy ground!
혻혻혻혻Holy ground for ever, stranger! All the quiet silver lights
혻혻혻혻Dropping from the starry heavens through the soft Australian nights 
혻혻혻혻Dropping on those lone grave-grasses come serene, unbroken, clear,
혻혻혻혻Like the love of God the Father, falling, falling, year by year!
혻혻혻혻Yea, and like a Voice supernal, there the daily wind doth blow
혻혻혻혻In the leaves above the sailor buried ninety years ago.
meaning:
Henry Kendall's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Sutherland占퐏 Grave"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
